mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 60%+ of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets, increasing consumer spending and corporate profit margins, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal monetary policy tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a monetary tightening cycle compresses risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of months where INDPRO rises by >1%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 900 thousand units signals strong demand for construction and related sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 900.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where both PAYEMS and UNRATE meet thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) during late-cycle expansion signals sustained demand pressures, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS and HOUST both rise by >2%.
